Numbers show the terrible consequences of other men's sins, yet carelessly take their places and follow the same destructive path. When we are in distress, we should desire the prayers of ministers and Christian friends. It is common for those who want to be prayed for not to accept advice; yet sinners are often hardened by a pause in judgments. But if God does not help us, no creature can. Whatever instruments God has decided to use, they will do the work, even though they seem unlikely.

There are times when it is wise for good men to withdraw, to enter their rooms, and shut the doors. Jeremiah was taken as a deserter and committed to prison. But it is nothing new for the best friends of the church to be lied about, in the interests of her worst enemies. When falsely accused like this, we may deny the charge and commit our cause to Him who judges righteously. Jeremiah obtained mercy from the Lord to remain faithful, and would not, to obtain mercy from people, be unfaithful to God or to his prince; he told the king the whole truth. When Jeremiah delivered God’s message, he spoke boldly; but when he made his own request, he spoke submissively. A lion in God's cause must be a lamb in his own. And God gave Jeremiah favor in the eyes of the king. The Lord God can make even prison cells into pastures for his people, and will raise up friends to provide for them, so that in times of famine they will be satisfied.
